notes: v1.14.97 - Fix: redesign bounced back to the legacy app (make redesign sticky). Root cause (reproduced via browser): in redesign, a Work launcher (deploy_quick/handoff_quick) opens the deploy workspace via showPage('sop'), which nav_push'es a LEGACY nav state; on BACK, popstate->nav_restore pops to the seeded root {p:'triage'} and showPage('triage') renders the legacy NOW page under the redesign frame. Fix: nav_restore now redesign-gates the bare root / default-legacy restore -> returns to the redesign frame via showMode(_rdMode) instead of a legacy page. Deeper flows (state.d) and the deploy dashboard (p:'sop' with an ops-tab/detail) stay valid, so internal deploy/handoff back-nav is unchanged. New _rdMode var (set in showMode) tracks the last redesign mode so back returns to Work, not Command. NOTE: deliberately did NOT suppress the launcher's nav_push (the handoff's _navInternalCall idea) - browser repro showed that removing the history entry breaks the back model (nothing to pop -> exits app / leaves Work); keeping the entry + the nav_restore guard is the correct, controlled return. Boot resume-restore was already redesign-guarded (restore() early-returns when flag on). All flag-gated; legacy (?redesign=0) byte-identical.
